title: First ecosystem-relationship teaching layer
summary: Seventh structured packet for Eco Explorer. The live beach, forest, and tundra biomes already contain real ecological relationships, but the runtime mostly teaches them one entry at a time. The recommended next step is a journal-first `ecosystem notes` layer: small authored biome notes that unlock when related discoveries have been made and that explain habitat roles or ecological processes without adding quizzes, large new UI, or AI dependence.

execution_order.goal: Prepare a small, science-accurate relationship-teaching slice for the live biome set.
execution_order.why_now: The game already has enough content depth that the next gain should come from connecting discoveries, not just adding more isolated facts.

execution_order.goal: Implement journal-first authored ecosystem notes backed by content data and a small pure helper.
execution_order.why_now: The journal is already the safest, most readable place to deepen meaning without crowding the first-contact inspect flow.

execution_order.goal: Review the first relationship layer for science accuracy, readability, and spoiler safety.
execution_order.why_now: This feature is only worthwhile if the science stays grounded and the note system feels supportive instead of noisy.

guardrails: Keep the first pass authored and deterministic. Do not make AI, clipboard, or network calls part of this feature.
Keep the UI journal-first. Do not add a separate overlay, companion, or world-map teaching screen in this pass.
Do not turn relationship teaching into a quiz, badge ladder, or achievement gate.
Do not reveal exact undiscovered species names when a note is still locked.
The relationship layer should deepen the selected entry instead of replacing its own journal teaching entirely.
Prefer ecological roles and processes already supported by the live content: shelter, dune stabilization, decomposition, seed movement, and cold-wind adaptation.
Keep the note text short enough to fit the existing 192x144 journal layout without reopening the readability problem.
acceptance_focus: At least one discovered journal entry in each live biome can surface an authored ecosystem note tied to related discoveries.
The relationship logic is data-driven enough to extend to ecotones and later biomes without hardcoded renderer-only strings.
The notes deepen ecosystem understanding while preserving the cozy exploration tone and hiding undiscovered exact names.
Tests protect unlock rules and the build remains green.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, docs/reports/2026-03-28-current-state-review.md, docs/reports/2026-03-28-ecosystem-relationship-scout.md, and this packet. Implement a small journal-first ecosystem-note system across beach, forest, and tundra. Keep it authored, deterministic, and spoiler-safe.
